title: Infer safety-car, VSC, red-flag, and weather-shift regimes from peer lap anomalies
summary: Public F1 strategy analysis says pit windows change sharply under Safety Car and low-grip tyre switches.
rationale: Public F1 strategy analysis says pit windows change sharply under Safety Car and low-grip tyre switches. The current best features are mostly row-local; they likely miss race-lap shocks visible only in cross-driver context.
implementation_hint: Build strictly causal race-lap aggregates such as median/quantile LapTime_Delta, median degradation change, fraction of drivers pitting on that lap, and deviations from each driver’s prior-lap baseline. Add short rolling shock flags and interactions with compound, tyre life, and position.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on opportunistic stops triggered by neutralizations or sudden grip/weather changes that row-only models mis-rank.
risk: If aggregates are not built causally within each race timeline, leakage is easy. Red-flag laps may also create noisy outliers.
